title: BigBear.ai Q1 2026 results: loss narrows, debt cut
text: BigBear.ai Q1 2026 results show improved financial metrics with narrowed losses and significantly reduced debt. The company reported a net loss of $56.8 million compared to $62.0 million in Q1 2025, representing a 8% improvement year-over-year. EPS loss of -$0.12 beat analyst expectations of -$0.08. Revenue remained relatively flat at $34.4 million versus $34.8 million in Q1 2025. The company's gross margin expanded dramatically to 34% from 21.3% in the prior year, driven by higher-margin generative AI products from the Ask Sage acquisition. Debt was cut substantially through the conversion of $124.6 million of 2029 Convertible Notes to equity in January 2026. Total debt now stands at only $16.5 million, due end of 2026. Cash and investments total $431.5 million, providing substantial financial flexibility. The company's Q1 10-Q filing provides detailed financial statements and management discussion and analysis regarding results, risks, and business strategy. Key SEC filings are available on BigBear.ai's investor relations website.
